unit: Unit 12: Everyday Life with Your Computer
title: QR Codes
drDigitalIntro:
QR codes are those square black-and-white patterns you see on menus, signs, and packaging. They're just links in disguise.

How to scan a QR code:
• On your phone, open the Camera app and point it at the QR code.
• A link appears at the top of the screen — tap it.
• That's it! The link opens in your browser.

Where you'll see them:
• Restaurant menus ("Scan here for our menu")
• Event tickets and boarding passes
• Product packaging (links to instructions or warranty info)
• Signs in stores or museums

Safety tip: only scan QR codes from sources you trust. A QR code on a random sticker stuck to a lamp post could lead anywhere — treat it like clicking a link from a stranger.
drDigitalSuccess: QR codes are just links you scan with your phone camera instead of typing. Quick and easy — just be smart about which ones you trust.
drDigitalHint: Just read through and click Continue.
